methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuantumDiamonds is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Kevin Berghoff and Fleming Bruckmaier. The sector includes 11 identified comparables—3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methods are not applicable here. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector peers, but none satisfy narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier), yielding fewer than the 3 minimum datapoints required for robust peer analysis. A fallback to all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given the heterogeneity in quantum modality, commercialization maturity, and market positioning across the peer set.

The absence of comparable seed-stage quantum companies in the dataset prevents triangulation via traditional multiples. QuantumDiamonds has raised €7M in seed capital plus a €152M facility commitment, but without documented comparable transaction multiples, revenue multiples, or development-stage benchmarks from peers, no quantitative valuation range can be defensibly derived. Any range assignment would require either (i) expansion of the comparable set to adjacent deep-tech or physics-based hardware stages, or (ii) application of venture cost-of-capital models using explicit discount rate assumptions. The primary uncertainty is the absence of precedent for seed valuations in this particular technical domain.
